item.title: Reports
item.description: DTEX publishes comprehensive insider risk management reports, from the cost of insider threats to nation-state operations and more.

item.title: Promod Haque
item.description: With more than 20 years of experience in venture capital, Promod serves as senior managing partner at Norwest Venture Partners (NVP).

item.title: Dave Salverson
item.description: As Chief Financial Officer at DTEX Systems, Dave develops the financial infrastructure to support DTEX’s growth trajectory.

item.title: Drew Jones
item.description: Drew Jones is the VP of Global Customer Success and Services at DTEX, leading worldwide strategies for customer engagement and retention.

item.title: Raman Hariharan
item.description: Raman Hariharan is the SVP of Engineering at DTEX Systems responsible for technology strategy and implementation of product roadmap.

item.title: Gaurav Garg
item.description: Gaurav Garg is a founding partner of Wing Venture Capital and has led investments in a number of early-stage technology companies.

item.title: Marc Kittrell
item.description: With 20 years of experience in IT software sales, Marc Kittrell is the VP of Worldwide Channels and Alliances for DTEX Systems.

item.title: Jessica Ray
item.description: As Chief Marketing Officer at DTEX, Jessica Ray leads all marketing initiatives with a rigorous focus on driving business growth.

item.title: Robert Lentz
item.description: With over 34 years of security experience in federal government, Mr. Lentz is an esteemed member of the DTEX Board of Directors.

item.title: Jennifer Moll
item.description: After 20 years of experience in forensics and cybersecurity, Jennifer Moll is the VP of Strategic Programs for DTEX Systems.

item.title: Puja Singhal
item.description: Puja Singhal is an HR leader with over 15 years of global experience in human resources, organizational development, and change management.

item.title: Louis Smith
item.description: Louis Smith is a cybersecurity executive with 15+ years in Sales & Engineering, driving DTEX's global growth and Insider Risk platform market leadership.

item.title: Christiaan Nillissen
item.description: With over 24 years in software, Chris co-founded DTEX Systems and has been pivotal in evolving the DTEX insider risk management platform.

item.title: Mohan Koo
item.description: Mohan Koo is the Co-founder and President of DTEX and is widely recognized as a thought leader in cybersecurity.

item.title: Chris Harris
item.description: Chris Harris is the Senior Vice President, United States Public Sector at DTEX, responsible for all aspects of public sector strategy.

item.title: Insider Risk Resolution: A Decision Tree Framework
item.description: The DTEX Insider Risk Resolution Tree can help analysts to make better decisions to proactively protect data and support a trusted workforce.

item.title: Gartner Insider Risk Management Market Guide: 4 Things You Need to Know
item.description: Gartner recently released their market guide for Insider Risk Management. Read our blog post for the four most important takeways.

item.title: Transforming Data Loss Prevention for an AI-Powered World
item.description: Learn how insider risk services help security teams accelerate insider threat investigations, cut containment times, and reduce breaches.

item.title: The Latest AI Security Risk: What OpenClaw Reveals About The Future of Agentic Malware
item.description: Learn how OpenClaw exposes catastrophic AI security risks from autonomous Shadow AI, and how you can keep your organization secure.

item.title: 4 Reasons to Ditch Your Old DLP in the AI-Era and What to Do Instead
item.description: Conventional DLP wasn’t built for AI-era. DTEX AI security capabilities and risk-adaptive DLP are the foundation for securing AI at scale.

item.title: Prompt and Circumstance: AI Is the New Attack Surface, and Browsers Enable It
item.description: Learn why legacy data loss prevention falls short for browser security, and how DTEX's risk-adaptive DLP delivers comprehensive protection.

item.title: How to Detect Unauthorized AI Agents: How OpenClaw Exposed the AI Insider Risk
item.description: This blog examines unauthorized AI agents and provides recommendations to mitigate their risks.

item.title: Ponemon Cost of Insider Risks: What’s Actually Working in the Age of Shadow AI
item.description: 2026 Ponemon Cost of Insider Risks Report shows why shadow AI is an insider threat problem, and how insider risk management is driving ROI.

item.title: The Security Stack Is Becoming an LLM. Insider Risk Still Isn’t.
item.description: The future security stack is going to be run by LLMs but it does not equal LLM-solves-security. Not safely. And not for insider risk.

item.title: Every Company Has a Roger: Risk-Adaptive DLP Catches Them
item.description: Risk-Adaptive DLP adapts to behavior, blocks risky GenAI prompts, and speeds investigations. See how DTEX protects data in real-time.

item.title: Insights From Australia’s Insider Risk Masterclass 2026
item.description: Takeaways from the 7th Insider Risk Masterclass, delivered through the AIRCoE: a collaboration between Aus3C, DTEX, McGrathNicol and MITRE.

item.title: AI Governance Best Practices: How to Balance Security with Innovation
item.description: Discover how to use AI governance to manage insider risk, control shadow AI, and safely scale enterprise innovation with visibility, context, and control.

item.title: The Real Quantum Risk in Finance: Data You Can’t See
item.description: Quantum cyber risk is already here. Learn how financial institutions can act early to protect against insider risks and unstructured data.

item.title: James Luo
item.description: James Luo co-leads CapitalG’s investing efforts in infrastructure software and focuses on security, networking, and developer platforms.

item.title: Negligent Insider Threat Tops News Radars, Walmart Insiders Get More Than They Bargained For
item.description: Bob Diachenko of Security Discovery revealed that he identified 800 million unprotected records stored in a 150GB-sized MongoDB instance online; Learn more here.
